title: Tender Smoky Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: Smoky and chewy Malaysian dish featuring marinated chicken, served with a side of spicy peanut sauce, this recipe is sugar free friendly and uses soda water for a unique twist.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, lime juice, vegetable oil, garlic, ginger, coriander, and cumin to make the marinade.
Add the chicken to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Brush the grill with oil to prevent sticking and cook the chicken for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, mix the peanut butter with soda water in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Deglaze the grill with a little water to get the smoky flavor.
Serve the chicken satay hot with the spicy peanut sauce for dipping.
Garnish with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired.
Leftovers can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
